symptoms.symptom_name: Empty, hypoplastic right hemiscrotum noticed by parents

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An empty hemiscrotum is the cardinal feature of an undescended or retractile testis.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: torsion of an undescended / inguinal testis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A newly empty hemiscrotum could reflect torsion of an inguinal testis and mandates exclusion by examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of pain, crying, scrotal redness or swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: torsion of an undescended / inguinal testis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic torsion is painful with an irritable child and a red, swollen, tender hemiscrotum; their absence lowers but does not exclude torsion.
A painless, quiet empty hemiscrotum fits a simple undescended/retractile testis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Feeding normally, settled, afebrile by parental report

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A well, feeding, afebrile infant is against incarceration/strangulation or systemic illness.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of a testis intermittently difficult to locate on that side

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A testis that comes and goes is characteristic of a retractile or intermittently ascending testis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth, mobile, non-tender testis palpable in the superficial inguinal pouch

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Locating a smooth, mobile, non-tender testis in the inguinal pouch confirms a palpable undescended/retractile testis — a clinician-only finding sealed from the AI Doctor.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: torsion of an undescended / inguinal testis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A non-tender, mobile testis is against torsion; only obtainable by palpation the AI Doctor cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brisk cremasteric reflex retracting the testis on attempted manipulation into the scrotum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A brisk cremasteric reflex indicates a retractile component; clinician-only, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Left testis descended and normal; no palpable inguinal hernia defect

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No reducible bowel loop or hernia defect on examination; clinician-only finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal infant vital signs (BP 90/55, HR 115, RR 28, SpO2 100%, Temp 36.8C, Wt 8 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, afebrile vitals are reassuring against acute systemic illness; clinician-measured values sealed from the AI Doctor, who has only the parents' report that the infant seems well.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scrotal / inguinal Doppler ultrasound assessing testicular position and blood flow

investigation_details.expected_result: Testis located in the inguinal canal/superficial pouch with preserved arterial flow (against torsion); used when examination is uncertain
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Clinical examination is first-line; ultrasound supplements it when the testis is impalpable or torsion is uncertain, but must not delay surgical assessment if torsion is suspected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: torsion of an undescended / inguinal testis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Doppler flow helps exclude torsion when clinical exam is equivocal; unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person genital/inguinal examination in a warm, relaxed setting
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line and definitive step to locate a viable, non-tender testis, distinguish retractile from undescended, and exclude torsion — the examination the AI Doctor cannot perform.
Supplementary if the testis is impalpable or torsion is clinically uncertain; not a substitute for urgent surgical assessment when torsion is suspected.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine paediatric urology / surgical referral for orchidopexy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive management of a confirmed undescended testis once torsion is excluded and the testis is confirmed viable.
